content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Tom Butt, Mayor 
City of Richmond 
450 Civic Center Plaza 
Richmond, Cali fomia 94804 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-128 
Dear Mr. Butt: 
July 6, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Refonn Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we arc only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section l 090. 
QUESTION 
Do you have a conflict of interest in any decisions related to the Tenninal 1 development 
project sponsored by Terminal One Development LLC due to the relationship between your firm, 
Interactive Resources, and Kwan Henmi Architects, the former architect for Terminal One 
Development LLC? 
CONCLUSION 
No. As Kwan Henmi Architects is no longer the architect employed by Terminal One 
Development, LLC, it is not reasonably foreseeable that decisions relating to the Tenninal 1 project 
will have any financial effects on your interests. Therefore, you do not have a conflict of interest. 
FACTS 
The Terminal l project is the development of approximately 13 .8 acres of property located 
on the San Francisco Bay in the City of Richmond, proposed by Terminal One Development LLC. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014 . All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18 I 10 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-128 
Page No. 2 
This property formerly served as a port terminal, and is presently owned by the City of Richmond, 
which is in contract to sell the site to Terminal One Development, LLC. Proposed development of 
the site includes residential and public open space uses. During the entitlement process and until 
May 2, 2016, Kwan Henmi Architects were the architects for Terminal One Development, LLC. As 
of May 2, 2016, Kwan Henmi Architects ceased being the architects for the Terminal I project. 
Kwan Henmi stopped all work on the project on that date and executed a transfer-release agreement 
with Termina! One Development, LLC on May 9, 2016. 
You are mayor of the City of Richmond and serve on the City Council. You are also the 
owner of more than 10 percent of Interactive Resources, Inc., an architecture-engineering firm 
located in Richmond. Interactive Resources is, and has been for some time, a sub-consultant to 
Kwan Henmi Architects for architecture-engineering services on BART projects and has received 
substantial compensation for this work. 
You have never received any compensation from Terminal One Development, LLC, or any 
other party connected with the Terminal I project. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's conflict-of-interest rules prohibit a public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position in any way to influence a governmental decision in 
which the official knows, or has reason to know, that he or she has a "financial interest." (Section 
87100.) A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87103 
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable 
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or 
on any one of five enumerated economic interests. (Section 87103.) 
Fi11a11cial /11/crcst 
According to your facts you have an interest in your architecture-engineering firm 
Interactive Resources, Inc., as an investment, and as a source of income. (Section 87103(a), (c) and 
(d).) Under the Act, income of an individual also includes a pro rata share of any income of a 
business entity in which the individual owns a ten percent interest or !:,'feater. Because you own ten 
percent or more of Interactive Resources, Inc., you are considered to be receiving "pass-through 
income" from the business's clients. Accordingly, you have an interest in Kwan Henrni Architects 
as a client of Interactive Resources that has paid you $500 or more in the past 12 months. (Section 
87103(c).) 
Foreseeczbility and Materiality 
A financial effect is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable when it is explicitly involved in 
a decision. Financial interests that are explicitly involved include an interest that is a named party 
in, or subject of, a government decision. (Regulation 18701 (a).) Neither Interactive Resources, Inc. 
nor Kwan Henmi Architects are explicitly involved in the decisions concerning the Terminal 1 
project. For interests that are not explicitly involved, as here, the effect need not be likely to be 
considered reasonably foreseeable ... In general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a 


File No. A-16-128 
Page No. 3 
realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable." 
(Regulation 18701 (b).) 
Under your facts, Kwan Henmi Architects, which is a source of income to you, is no longer 
involved in the Tenninal I project. In addition, your business Interactive Resources, Inc., does not 
have any other involvement in the project or to parties connected to the project. Therefore, it is not 
reasonably foreseeable that a decision concerning the Tenninal I project will have a material 
financial effect on these financial interests of yours. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ZWN: jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
Zachary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question:
Do you have a conflict of interest in any decisions related to the Tenninal 1 development 
project sponsored by Terminal One Development LLC due to the relationship between your firm, 
Interactive Resources, and Kwan Henmi Architects, the former architect for Terminal One 
Development LLC?
sections.conclusion:
No. As Kwan Henmi Architects is no longer the architect employed by Terminal One 
Development, LLC, it is not reasonably foreseeable that decisions relating to the Tenninal 1 project 
will have any financial effects on your interests. Therefore, you do not have a conflict of interest.
